steps.title: Find the passage's central action
steps.content: The pivotal sentence is "This affectionate willingness to fall in with his own plans quite won the old man's heart." Adam notices his great-uncle's interest in the ship, offers to stay the night, and agrees to go anywhere at any hour — and that consideration directly transforms the meeting into something "almost like old friends." The text is fundamentally about how Adam's behavior shapes the relationship.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Work through each choice against the passage:
  • Choice A — 'It states the reasons why Adam and his great-uncle Richard decide to sleep on the ship rather than finding lodging on land.': The text never mentions lodging on land as an alternative, and the sleeping arrangement is a minor example, not the main point. ✗
  • Choice B — 'It showcases how Adam's flexibility and consideration strengthen his relationship with his great-uncle Richard.': This matches exactly — the passage shows Adam's accommodating behavior winning the old man's heart and deepening the bond. ✓
  • Choice C — 'It describes why Adam and his great-uncle Richard are excited for their upcoming journey on the ship.': The text provides no details about a planned journey or any excitement about travel; it focuses on the developing relationship. ✗
  • Choice D — 'It contrasts great-uncle Richard's wary first impressions of Adam with his ultimate affection toward him.': The meeting begins "auspiciously" and proceeds well from the start — there is no initial wariness. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
